facts.text: The Simpsons began as a series of animated shorts on The Tracey Ullman Show in 1987 before becoming its own series.
The Simpsons premiered as a half-hour primetime show on Fox on December 17, 1989.
The Simpsons is the longest-running American animated series and the longest-running primetime scripted TV show in U.S. history.
Matt Groening named Homer, Marge, Lisa, and Maggie after members of his own family, while Bart's name is an anagram of 'brat'.
Dan Castellaneta voices Homer Simpson as well as characters such as Krusty the Clown, Groundskeeper Willie, and Barney Gumble.
Julie Kavner voices Marge Simpson and Marge's sisters Patty and Selma.
Nancy Cartwright voices Bart Simpson, whose spiky-haired design became one of the most recognizable silhouettes in television.
Yeardley Smith has voiced Lisa Simpson since the beginning of the series.
The family was colored bright yellow partly so channel surfers would instantly notice the show while flipping through TV stations.
The opening theme for The Simpsons was composed by Danny Elfman, who wrote the famous melody in just a few days.
The location of Springfield is intentionally ambiguous, and the show has joked for decades about never revealing exactly which state it is in.
The phrase 'D'oh!' became so popular through Homer Simpson that it was eventually added to major dictionaries.
The annual 'Treehouse of Horror' episodes let the show parody horror, sci-fi, and fantasy stories outside normal continuity.
The Simpsons has parodied everything from Citizen Kane and The Godfather to Twin Peaks and Game of Thrones.
Bartmania swept pop culture in the early 1990s, with Bart Simpson shirts, toys, and catchphrases becoming a huge merchandising phenomenon.
The catchphrase 'Eat my shorts' became one of Bart Simpson's earliest signature lines.
Homer's job is safety inspector at the Springfield Nuclear Power Plant, owned by the ruthless C. Montgomery Burns.
Mr. Burns and Smithers form one of the most iconic boss-assistant duos in TV comedy.
The 1995 cliffhanger 'Who Shot Mr. Burns?' became one of the biggest TV mysteries of the decade.
Maggie Simpson's first spoken word in the series was voiced by Elizabeth Taylor.
Kelsey Grammer voices Sideshow Bob, one of the show's most beloved recurring villains.
The fake cartoon 'Itchy & Scratchy' is a deliberately over-the-top parody of violent cat-and-mouse animation like Tom and Jerry.
The Simpsons helped establish Fox as a major television network in the early 1990s.
The show won a Peabody Award in 1996 for its cultural impact and sharp satire.
The Simpsons Movie was released on July 27, 2007, and grossed more than $500 million worldwide.
Spider-Pig, introduced in The Simpsons Movie, quickly became one of the franchise's biggest running jokes.
The show's famous couch gags allow the animators to reinvent the opening sequence in new ways for different episodes.
Guest animators and filmmakers including Banksy, Guillermo del Toro, and Don Hertzfeldt have created special Simpsons couch gags.
The Simpsons switched to high-definition animation in 2009 while keeping its classic visual style.
Hank Azaria voices many fan-favorite supporting characters including Moe, Chief Wiggum, Comic Book Guy, and Apu.
Harry Shearer voices Mr. Burns, Ned Flanders, Principal Skinner, Kent Brockman, and many other Springfield residents.
Ned Flanders became such a strong stereotype of cheerful religiosity that the term 'Flanderization' grew out of how exaggerated TV characters can become over time.
Lisa Simpson's saxophone solos became a recurring part of the show's emotional core and jazz identity.
The Simpsons has featured hundreds of celebrity guest voices, from Paul McCartney and Leonard Nimoy to Meryl Streep and Lady Gaga.
Many fans love the so-called 'Golden Age' of The Simpsons, often identified as the run from the early 1990s through the late 1990s.
The show has joked so often about future technology and politics that it developed a reputation for 'predicting' real-world events.
A 1998 episode joking about Disney owning Fox became especially famous after Disney actually bought most of 21st Century Fox decades later.
The Simpsons has produced more than 750 episodes, a milestone no other American scripted primetime series had reached first.
Milhouse Van Houten was named after U.S. president Richard Nixon, whose middle name was Milhous.
Krusty the Clown was originally conceived as Homer's secret clown identity before the idea was dropped during development.
The town of Springfield contains recurring locations like Moe's Tavern, Kwik-E-Mart, the Android's Dungeon comic shop, and the Springfield Elementary School.
The Simpsons' satirical take on suburbia, politics, religion, and media helped redefine what animated television could do for adults.
Fox repeatedly renewed The Simpsons deep into the 2020s, extending its own longevity record again and again.
The show's influence can be seen across later adult animation, including Family Guy, South Park, Futurama, Bob's Burgers, and Rick and Morty.
Matt Groening created The Simpsons and based several family names on his own relatives.
That personal starting point helped launch one of television's biggest cultural institutions.
